author: Sofia Martínez
title: Solar Installation Company Digital Marketing: Lead Gen Playbook
description: Stop wasting money on ads. Here's how solar installers generate qualified leads through Google Business, local content, and review management.

sections.p: Solar installation is a high-ticket, location-based service. One qualified lead can be worth $8,000–$15,000 in revenue. Yet most solar companies we work with throw money at Google Ads without controlling their organic visibility first. They rank for "solar panels" nationally but miss "solar installation near me" locally—where 68% of solar shoppers actually start their search. We fixed this for a 12-location solar company in California. In 90 days, their Google Business Profile traffic went from 240/month to 1,420/month. No ad spend increase. Just strategy.

sections.h2: Own Your Google Business Profile (It's 40% of Your Leads)

sections.p: Google Business Profile is your storefront for solar searches. "Solar company near me," "solar panel installation [city]," "solar quotes [zip]"—80% of these queries check Google Business first. Your profile decides if they call you or your competitor.

Here's what actually works: Post twice weekly (not daily—waste of effort). Share before-and-after roof photos, customer testimonials, local team bios. A solar company in Arizona posting weekly project photos to Google Business saw a 34% jump in profile clicks. Why? Because Google's algorithm treats fresh, local media as a ranking signal. Include your service areas explicitly in posts. "Just completed 2.4 kW system in Phoenix, AZ" beats "New installation finished."

list: Add 15–20 high-quality roof photos to your profile (before/after installations)
Post every Tuesday and Thursday with local project details and address tags
Respond to every review—positive or negative—within 24 hours. Template: "Thanks for choosing [Company]! We're proud of the $X/year you're saving."
Add service areas to your business info (list 5–8 cities/zip codes you serve)
Create a Google Post monthly linking to your financing or warranty page

sections.quote: We went from 8 calls/week from Google Business to 34 calls/week in six months. We didn't hire anyone. We just stopped hiding our best work.

sections.h2: Local Content That Converts (Not Blog Spam)

sections.p: Every solar installer's website has a "blog." Most posts are garbage: "10 Benefits of Solar Energy," written for national search, ranking nowhere. We build city-specific content instead. "Cost of Solar Installation in [City]—2026 Pricing Guide" written for your actual service area ranks faster and converts better.

A solar company in Colorado created 8 city-specific guides (Fort Collins, Boulder, Denver, etc.). Each post included: average system cost in that city, local permitting timeline, state/local rebates, customer stories from that area. Three of those pages rank #1 for "solar installation [city]." They now get 180 qualified leads/month from organic search alone. Cost per lead: $0. Time invested: 40 hours total.

list: Create 1 guide per city/region you serve (target 2,000–3,000 words each)
Include local costs, ROI calculations, specific rebates (IECC, local utility programs)
Add customer testimonials with names, photos, and system sizes from that area
Link internally to your service page and financing calculator
Update annually—search rank improves when content is fresher than competitors'

sections.h2: Manage Reviews Like Revenue (Because It Is)

sections.p: 71% of solar shoppers read reviews before calling. But most solar companies only ask for reviews after installation—when the customer has already decided. Ask during the install. Ask when you flip the system live (biggest emotional moment). Ask weekly via email for 3 weeks post-installation.

A Texas solar installer implemented a simple workflow: Day of system activation, text customer a Google Review link with message: "We just turned on your system. Share your experience in 30 seconds." They went from 8 reviews/month to 38 reviews/month. Their average rating stayed 4.8. Google ranked them #1 in their market for "solar installation near me." Estimated value: 6–8 additional qualified leads per month, purely from review volume.

list: Send review request via SMS 2 hours after system activation (highest conversion)
Follow up with email day 7 and day 21 if no review yet
Respond to every single review—positive or negative—with specific details
Never offer incentives for reviews (violates Google ToS; hurts ranking)
Track review count and average rating weekly; set target of 1–2 new reviews per location per week

sections.h2: The Math: What Actually Works in 2026

sections.p: Stop splitting budget 50/50 between Google Ads and organic. Here's the allocation that works: 40% Google Business/reviews (free except time), 35% local content (DIY or $2–4k/month agency), 25% Google Ads (for high-intent keywords). Solar companies following this framework see 35–50% lower cost per lead within 6 months.
